HFCL: APPROVES ₹215 CRORE AI DATA CENTRE CONNECTIVITY PRODUCTS FACILITY 🏭🚀

• 🏗️ HFCL's Board has approved setting up a state-of-the-art manufacturing facility for Data Center Connectivity Products with an annual capacity of 2.7 lakh assemblies at an estimated capital outlay of ₹215 crore. 

• 🤖 The investment is driven by the surging global demand for AI, hyperscale data centres, cloud computing, high-performance computing and high-speed networking, creating significant business opportunities in domestic and international markets. 

• 🔌 The facility will manufacture Miniature Multi-Fiber (MMC) and Super High-Density Multi-Fiber Termination (SNMT) assemblies used in high-speed data centres and AI infrastructure and is expected to be commissioned by September 2027.  

• 🌍 The project will be funded through an appropriate mix of internal accruals and debt and is expected to strengthen manufacturing capabilities, expand exports, support import substitution and reinforce HFCL's position in next-generation optical connectivity solutions. 

🟢 Impact: Positive (The ₹215 crore expansion strengthens HFCL's presence in the fast-growing AI and data centre connectivity market, creating long-term growth opportunities.)

BONDADA ENGINEERING: CRISIL UPGRADES LONG-TERM RATING TO A+/STABLE 📈🏦

• 🏆 Bondada Engineering has received a CRISIL credit rating upgrade, with its Long-Term Rating upgraded to CRISIL A+/Stable from CRISIL A/Stable, while the Short-Term Rating has been reaffirmed at CRISIL A1. 

• 💰 The company's rated banking facilities have increased sharply from ₹800 crore to ₹2,000 crore, reflecting its expanding scale of operations and stronger banking relationships. 

• 📊 CRISIL cited the company's strong financial profile, consistent execution capabilities, healthy order book, prudent financial management and strong liquidity position as key factors supporting the rating upgrade. 

• 🤝 The upgraded rating is expected to strengthen Bondada's credibility with lenders, financial institutions, customers and business partners, supporting future growth initiatives. 

🟢 Impact: Positive (The credit rating upgrade enhances Bondada Engineering's financial credibility, improves access to funding and supports long-term business expansion.)

MAHINDRA LIFESPACES: EXPANDS CHENNAI INDUSTRIAL PARK JV WITH SUMITOMO 🏭🤝

• 🤝 Mahindra Lifespace's subsidiary Mahindra World City Developers (MWCDL), Sumitomo Corporation, Japan, and Mahindra Industrial Park Chennai (MIPCL) have signed a Second Supplemental Agreement to expand their existing industrial park partnership.  

• 🏗️ The agreement provides for the development of Phase 2B of the Chennai Industrial Park in Tamil Nadu, extending the existing joint venture under which Phase 1 and Phase 2A were developed. 

• 📊 Mahindra Lifespaces holds 89% stake in MWCDL, while MWCDL owns 60% of MIPCL and Sumitomo Corporation holds the remaining 40%. 

• 🌏 Under the agreement, MWCDL will continue to nominate the majority of directors on MIPCL's board, with the collaboration aimed at further expanding the industrial and manufacturing ecosystem in Chennai. 

🟢 Impact: Positive (The expansion of the Chennai industrial park strengthens Mahindra Lifespaces' industrial real estate portfolio and supports long-term growth through continued collaboration with Sumitomo.)

AMEENJI RUBBER: BAGS ₹4.20 CRORE SOUTHERN RAILWAY PURCHASE ORDER 🚆📦

• 📜 Ameenji Rubber has received a purchase order worth ₹4.20 crore from Southern Railway for the manufacture and supply of 6 mm Thick Nylon Cord Reinforced Grooved Rubber Sole Plates conforming to RDSO specifications.  

• 🏭 The order involves manufacturing and supplying the rubber sole plates along with all applicable technical requirements and amendments for Southern Railway. 

• ⏳ The contract will be executed as per the phased delivery schedule, with the rate contract valid up to March 31, 2027. 

• 🤝 The order has been awarded by a domestic entity, with no promoter interest and no related-party transaction involved. 

🟢 Impact: Positive (The ₹4.20 crore railway order strengthens Ameenji Rubber's order book, improves revenue visibility and reinforces its presence in the railway infrastructure segment.)

SWOJAS FOODS: CONVERTS 16.16 LAKH WARRANTS INTO EQUITY SHARES 📈💰

• 📝 Swojas Foods' Board has approved the conversion of 16,16,162 warrants into 16,16,162 fully paid-up equity shares pursuant to the exercise of conversion rights by a non-promoter investor. 

• 💵 The company received the remaining ₹2 crore (approximately ₹2,00,00,004.75) towards the 75% balance warrant exercise price at ₹12.375 per warrant. 

• 📊 Following the conversion, the company's paid-up equity share capital has increased from 4.26 crore shares to 4.43 crore shares. 

• 🤝 The conversion was exercised by Onelife Capital Advisors Limited, while 67.84 lakh warrants remain outstanding for future conversion. 

🟢 Impact: Positive (The warrant conversion brings fresh capital into the company, strengthens its equity base and reflects investor commitment.)

Biopharma SHAKTI Scheme | Key Highlights & Potential Beneficiaries

Scheme Overview
- Outlay of ₹10,000 crore.
- Five-year implementation period.
- Strengthen India's biopharma ecosystem.
- Focus on biologics and biosimilars.
- Improve global competitiveness.
- Promote affordable healthcare.

Key Focus Areas
- Biopharma discovery funding.
- Equity support for innovation.
- National Biopharma R&D Network.
- NIPER biopharma ecosystem.
- Fermentation-based bulk drugs.
- Biologics manufacturing incentives.
- Drug delivery & packaging ecosystem.
- Biosimilars manufacturing support.

Clinical Research Push
- 1,000 accredited clinical trial sites.
- Led by ICMR.
- Faster clinical development.
- Strengthen CDSCO capabilities.
- Dedicated scientific reviewers.
- Better regulatory efficiency.

Expected Industry Impact
- Higher R&D investments.
- Stronger domestic manufacturing.
- Reduced import dependence.
- Faster biologics development.
- Boost to CRDMO industry.
- Increased global outsourcing.
- Enhanced innovation ecosystem.

Potential Beneficiaries
- Biocon – Biosimilars & biologics leader.
- Syngene International – CRDMO & biologics research.
- Laurus Labs – CDMO & biotechnology expansion.
- Divi's Laboratories – Custom synthesis & APIs.
- Aurobindo Pharma – Biosimilars & injectables.
- Dr. Reddy's Laboratories – Biosimilars & biologics pipeline.

Most Direct Beneficiaries
- Syngene International.
- Biocon.
- Laurus Labs.

Impact
- Strong Positive.
- Long-term structural policy support.
- Encourages innovation-led growth.
- Strengthens India's global biopharma position.

Key Takeaway
- The ₹10,000 crore Biopharma SHAKTI Scheme is a landmark policy aimed at building India's biologics and biosimilars ecosystem through funding, manufacturing incentives, clinical research infrastructure and regulatory reforms. Companies with strong capabilities in biologics, biosimilars and CRDMO services—particularly Biocon, Syngene International, Laurus Labs, Divi's Laboratories, Aurobindo Pharma and Dr. Reddy's Laboratories—are well positioned to benefit from this multi-year structural growth opportunity.

INFOSYS: FOUNDATION COMMITS ₹147 CRORE TO PGIMER MOTHER & CHILD CENTRE 🏥❤️

• 🏥 The Infosys Foundation has supported the Advanced Mother and Child Centre (AMCC) at PGIMER, Chandigarh, with a commitment of over ₹147 crore towards critical medical equipment. 

• 🇮🇳 The facility was inaugurated by Prime Minister Narendra Modi and is expected to benefit over 25,000 pregnant women and newborns annually by strengthening maternal and neonatal healthcare. 

• 🩺 The centre includes labour and delivery units, neonatal ICU, obstetric ICU, modular operation theatres, blood bank, human milk bank and emergency care facilities. 

• 🤝 The initiative is part of Infosys Foundation's CSR and healthcare mission, aimed at improving access to quality and affordable healthcare for underserved communities. 

🟡 Impact: Neutral (This is a CSR initiative that enhances Infosys' social impact and brand reputation but has no material impact on its financial performance.)

APOLLO MICRO SYSTEMS: WINS INDIAN NAVY MAKE-II PROTOTYPE SANCTION ORDER ⚓🇮🇳

• 🛡️ Apollo Micro Systems has been awarded a Make-II Prototype Sanction Order (PSO) by the Indian Navy for its indigenous Naval Maritime Electronic Warfare capabilities. 

• 🚤 The project involves the design, development and prototype demonstration of SAVIOR-ASW (Semi-Submersible Autonomous Vessel for Intelligence, Operations and Reconnaissance) under the Make-II category. 

• 🤖 SAVIOR-ASW is an unmanned semi-submersible platform for persistent Anti-Submarine Warfare (ASW) surveillance, featuring AI/ML-based target classification, advanced acoustic sensors and encrypted multi-channel communication systems. 

• 🚀 The company said the order marks its formal entry into autonomous maritime and underwater warfare systems. Under the Make-II framework, the Government has no cost obligation during prototype development, with procurement envisaged upon successful prototype demonstration. 

🟢 Impact: Positive (The prestigious Indian Navy Make-II order strengthens Apollo Micro Systems' defence portfolio, enhances its position in next-generation naval technologies and opens long-term procurement opportunities.)

• 🤝 Bharat Forge has signed a strategic MoU with French-Canadian aerospace company FLYING WHALES to jointly develop, manufacture and deploy heavy-lift airships for India's defence and strategic applications. 

• 🇮🇳 The partnership aims to establish Make in India production of next-generation airships, supporting the Aatmanirbhar Bharat initiative by building an indigenous airship ecosystem for sovereign defence capabilities. 

• ✈️ The collaboration will focus on the LCA60T heavy-lift airship, capable of carrying up to 60 tonnes of cargo with vertical take-off and landing (VTOL) capability, hybrid-electric propulsion and minimal ground infrastructure requirements. 

• 🛡️ The airships are expected to support military logistics, transport of oversized equipment, ISR missions, communication relay, disaster relief and rapid deployment in remote border regions, while also creating high-technology jobs, an advanced supply chain and export opportunities. 

🟢 Impact: Positive (The partnership strengthens Bharat Forge's aerospace and defence portfolio, creates a new indigenous manufacturing opportunity and enhances long-term growth in strategic defence technologies.)

Cyient DLM: Guidance - - Concall Update

• Management expects to maintain a book-to-bill ratio of 1.5x for the full year, following a robust 1.5x in Q1 FY '27, which contributed to the highest-ever order book of INR 2,598 crores.

• The company anticipates continued strong revenue momentum for FY '27, with similar trends expected in Q2 and beyond, positioning for sustained growth throughout the year.

• EBITDA margins are projected to expand from the current 9-11% in the "Strengthen" phase to 11-13% in the "Expand" phase (FY '27-'29) and further to 13-18% in the "Transform" phase (FY '30 and beyond).

• New segments like AI data centers and robotics are expected to see order intake this year, with revenue contributions primarily in FY '28 and FY '29, and the semiconductor capital equipment segment is anticipated to show revenue growth in the next 6 to 12 months.

• The company does not foresee needing additional CapEx beyond regular annual spending, as existing capabilities are largely sufficient for expansion into new segments.

MOTHERSON: COMPLETES ACQUISITION OF 81% STAKE IN JAPAN'S YUTAKA GIKEN 🇯🇵🤝

• 🤝 Samvardhana Motherson has completed the acquisition of an 81% stake in Yutaka Giken Co., Ltd. (YGCL) through its indirect wholly owned subsidiary Motherson Global Investments B.V. (MGI BV). 

• 🏭 As part of the transaction, Yutaka Giken completed the buyback of shares held by Honda Motor, resulting in 81% ownership by MGI BV and 19% by Honda Motor. 

• 📅 The final closing of the transaction was completed on July 21, 2026, following all required approvals and completion of conditions. 

• 🌍 Yutaka Giken is a Japan-based listed automotive component manufacturer, while its subsidiary Shinnichi Kogyo is also part of the acquired business, strengthening Motherson's global automotive portfolio. 

🟢 Impact: Positive (The acquisition strengthens Motherson's global automotive components business, expands its presence in Japan and supports long-term growth through portfolio diversification.)

PARAS DEFENCE: SIGNS MoU FOR ₹6,200 CRORE SEMICONDUCTOR OSAT FACILITY IN MADHYA PRADESH 🏭🇮🇳

• 🤝 Paras Defence's subsidiary Paras Semiconductors Pvt. Ltd. has signed an MoU with MP State Electronics Development Corporation (MPSeDC) to establish a greenfield advanced IC packaging OSAT (Outsourced Semiconductor Assembly and Test) facility in Madhya Pradesh. 

• 💰 The proposed project involves an investment of approximately ₹6,200 crore and will be set up in the Indore–Ujjain region of Madhya Pradesh. 

• ⚙️ The facility will undertake advanced semiconductor packaging operations, including 3D heterogeneous integration, 2.5D/3D packaging, hybrid bonding, ultra-high-density fan-out packaging, chiplet integration, wafer bumping, flip-chip assembly, testing and reliability. 

• 🚀 The collaboration aims to combine the expertise of Paras Semiconductors and MPSeDC to strengthen India's semiconductor manufacturing ecosystem through advanced IC packaging capabilities. 

🟢 Impact: Positive (The proposed ₹6,200 crore OSAT facility significantly strengthens Paras Defence's semiconductor ambitions, creating a long-term growth opportunity in India's strategic electronics manufacturing ecosystem.)

TRUMP'S GENERIC DRUG TARIFF PLAN: WHAT IT MEANS FOR INDIAN PHARMA

• 💊 0% tariff on imported generic drugs from Aug 1, 2026 for the next 2 years, providing near-term relief to exporters

• 🏭 After the 2-year window, tariffs proposed to rise to 100% for one year, followed by 200% thereafter, encouraging companies to manufacture in the US

• ⏳ The two-year transition period gives Indian pharma companies time to reassess supply chains and accelerate US manufacturing investments

• 📈 Near-term beneficiaries: Aurobindo Pharma, Dr. Reddy's Laboratories and Lupin, as exports and pricing remain largely unaffected in the immediate future

• ⚠️ Long-term: Companies with significant US generic exposure may need to expand local manufacturing to avoid steep tariffs from 2028 onward

🟢 Near-term Impact: Positive for Indian generic exporters.

🟠 Long-term Impact: Mixed, depending on each company's US manufacturing footprint.

Tatva Chintan | Semiconductor Chemicals Opportunity

India's Semiconductor Push
- India investing ₹1.64 lakh crore in chip fabs.
- Supply chain remains largely import dependent.
- 85–90% semiconductor chemicals imported.
- Most equipment also sourced overseas.

Technical Challenge
- Semiconductor chemicals require ultra-high purity.
- Impurities measured in parts per billion.
- Tiny contamination can destroy entire chip batches.
- Supplier qualification is lengthy and stringent.

Tatva Chintan's Progress
- Developing five semiconductor-grade chemicals.
- One product cleared factory-level qualification tests.
- Commercial sales still around two years away.
- Three to four additional qualification rounds pending.

Commercialization Hurdles
- Chipmakers rarely switch qualified suppliers.
- MNC distributors validate and market products.
- Qualification process spans several years.
- Reliability matters more than pricing.

Industry Perspective
- Domestic fabs are being established.
- Supporting chemical ecosystem remains underdeveloped.
- Industry estimates suggest $80 billion support needed by 2035.
- Supply-chain localization will be gradual.

Key Takeaway
- Tatva Chintan has achieved an important technical milestone by qualifying its first semiconductor-grade chemical, but meaningful commercialization is still a multi-year process. India's semiconductor opportunity is significant, yet building a domestic chemicals ecosystem will require time, capital, and successful qualification across the entire supply chain.

GENERIC PHARMA TARIFF ANNOUNCEMENT BY TRUMP

1) All generic drugs being brought into the US will continue having ‌a tariff of 0% for two years starting from August 1. 

2) After 2 years the tariff rate will be raised to 100% for one year and to 200% thereafter.

Analysts Say-

1) Aurobindo has already build a substantial local manufacturing presence hence it can benefit from this, if implemented.

2) Dr. Reddy's, Lupin, Cipla and Zydus have some manufacturing footprint in the US, but that can not be called meaningful enough hence can get impacted hence this is sentimentally negative.

3) Alkem and Torrent are fully dependent on Indian manufacturing units, but they don't generate enough cashflows from US generics hence neutral impact.

4) Biocon is also depending on India and Malasian units for biosimilar and generic manufacturing and may see sentimentally negative impact.

5) Senores Pharma can benefit due to their local manufacturing presence for US generics market. 

In conclusion, this is difficult to implement policy as 90% of the US market by volumes is generic in nature. Aurobindo and Senores can benefit, if implemented and other large players may have to invest in the US and may see sentimentally negative impact.

PHARMA STOCKS ;  TRUMP ANNOUNCES PHASED TARIFF PLAN FOR GENERIC DRUG IMPORTS

• 💊 Generic drug imports to the US to continue at 0% tariff from Aug. 1, 2026 for 2 years

• ⏳ Tariff to rise to 100% for 1 year, followed by 200% thereafter

• 🏭 Aim is to encourage generic drug manufacturers to set up production in the US

• ⚕️ Policy does not apply to patented, branded or innovative drugs

🇮🇳 Likely Impact on Indian Pharma

🟢 Near-term (Positive):

No immediate tariff impact for the next two years, giving Indian exporters business continuity.

Companies with strong US generic exposure such as Sun Pharma, Dr. Reddy's, Aurobindo Pharma, Lupin, Cipla, Zydus Lifesciences, Glenmark get time to plan.

🔴 Long-term (Negative if implemented):

Exporters without US manufacturing could face significantly higher tariffs after the transition period.

Companies with existing or planned US manufacturing facilities would be relatively better positioned.

• 📈 Total waste managed grew 5.4% YoY to ~1.40 million tonnes, while core revenue increased ~7% YoY

• 🚛 Collection & Transportation volumes rose 5.1% YoY to ~0.55 million tonnes, while Processing volumes increased 5.5% YoY to ~0.85 million tonnes

• 🏭 Successfully refinanced PCMC Waste-to-Energy plant loan, reducing interest rate from 10.25% to 8.25%, lowering financing costs by 200 bps

• 🌧️ Following the July 8 natural calamity at the PCMC WtE facility, 9 employees lost their lives after a landfill collapse outside the plant boundary

• 🤝 Company announced ₹25 lakh financial assistance per deceased employee, employment for eligible next of kin, education support for children, and medical care for injured employees

• ✅ MRF and composting plant are fully operational, while phased restart of the remaining facility is underway

🟡 Impact: Mixed (Core operations remain resilient with volume growth and lower financing costs, but the natural calamity may temporarily impact WtE operations and sentiment.)

ACCIDENT TOMORROW: BANDHAN BANK ? 

GUIDANCE CUT 

It has done 1% RoA in Q1FY27 but has cut exit Q4FY27 RoA guidance to 1.2-1.4% (vs 1.6-1.8% earlier). The cut is primarily due to higher opex (tech spends, LOS etc) and stable NIM (vs improvement earlier).

*RoA guidance revised downwards*

- Management _*cut FY27 exit Q4FY27 RoA guidance from 1.6-1.8% to 1.2-1.4%*_ due to the challenging external environment like elevated funding cost, energy crisis due to West Asia war, unpredictable monsoons and higher tech costs.

- The ~40bps cut in guided RoA was attributed to NIM (which now looks stable) and ~10bps impact on opex. Opex is towards tech spends etc.

- Maintain growth guidance of 14% YoY for FY27

- *NIM outlook*: NIM of the bank was stable QoQ at 6.2% as against earlier expectation of NIM expansion, which the management attributed to elevated funding costs.

Atlanta Electricals Ltd Q1FY27  
 
➤ Revenue ₹466.33 Cr (+48.0% YoY)

➤ EBITDA ₹77.10 Cr (+58.1% YoY)
EBITDA Margin 16.5% (+105 bps YoY)

➤ PAT ₹46.84 Cr (+50.4% YoY)
PAT Margin 10.0% (+16 bps YoY)

➤ Revenue growth was driven by healthy execution, higher capacity utilization, and strong demand from the power transmission & distribution and renewable energy sectors

➤ Order Book ₹3,116.63 Cr as of June 30, 2026 (+25.0% QoQ), with Q1 order inflows of ₹792.42 Cr, providing strong revenue visibility

➤ Business mix continues to shift towards high-capacity transformers, with products rated 220 kV and above contributing a larger share of revenue

➤ Over 55% of the order book comprises 220 kV transformers, while 400 kV transformers and reactors contributed over ₹275 Cr in new orders

➤ Secured a ₹291.68 Cr order from Rajasthan Rajya Vidyut Prasaran Nigam Ltd. (RVPNL) for multiple high-capacity transformers

➤ Continued strengthening capabilities in EHV and UHV transformers, with approvals for 400 kV class transformers and ongoing investments to address opportunities in transmission, renewable energy, BESS, data centres, and exports

➤ Management Commentary:-
✓ Q1 FY27 started on a strong note with 48.0% YoY revenue growth, 58.1% YoY EBITDA growth, and 50.4% YoY PAT growth, driven by expanded manufacturing capacity, disciplined execution, and improved operating leverage

✓ Order book increased 25% QoQ to ₹3,116.63 Cr, providing strong revenue visibility and reflecting robust demand across transmission & distribution, renewable energy, and industrial applications

✓ Business mix is steadily shifting towards higher-capacity transformers, increasing the share of premium products in both the order book and revenue, supporting long-term earnings quality

✓ Management remains optimistic about long-term growth, supported by India's expanding power infrastructure, transmission network investments, renewable energy integration, battery energy storage systems (BESS), industrial electrification, and data centre demand

✓ Company continues to focus on higher capacity utilisation, expanding exports, commissioning its Inverter Duty Transformer facility, progressing backward integration, and strengthening capabilities in EHV/UHV transformers, including 400 kV and 765 kV products

Legal Update
- ONGC seeks enforcement of $307 million arbitral award.

- Next hearing on 11 September 2026.

Company's Position
- Amount already provided for in books.
- No interim adverse order passed.
- Evaluating legal remedies.

Key Takeaway
- The arbitration enforcement petition represents an ongoing legal matter for Vedanta Oil & Gas, with no immediate operational impact as the company has already provided for the amount in its financial statements.

How inflection reflects in financial return ratios of a business?

Following is the example of KVB:

1. ROE expanded from 16% to 18% almost.
2. ROA went from 1.63% to a run rate of 2.11%
3. Net interest margins have improved non stop over last 5 Qs.
4. Cost to income has fallen thereby reflecting operating leverage in the business.

These inflection lasts for 2-3 years before new opex has to be incurred by a business to grow further.

Just a small example on how infections occur.

Keep tracking improvement in return ratios over every Quarterly to semi annual and annual results

JSW Infrastructure | Q1FY27 Update

Financial Highlights
- Cargo volumes 31 MT (+6% YoY).
- Operating revenue ₹1,445 crore (+18% YoY).
- Operating EBITDA ₹674 crore (+16% YoY).
- Ports revenue ₹1,208 crore (+11% YoY).
- Logistics revenue ₹237 crore.
- Logistics EBITDA ₹73 crore (3.6x YoY).
- PAT ₹358 crore (vs ₹390 crore).

FY27 Guidance
- Operating revenue target ₹6,850 crore.
- Operating EBITDA target ₹3,000 crore.
- EBITDA growth expected around 15%.
- FY28 EBITDA to nearly double vs FY26.

Growth Strategy
- Cargo capacity target 400 MTPA by FY30.
- Current capacity 186 MTPA.
- ₹30,000 crore port expansion plan.
- ₹9,000 crore logistics investment.
- Navkar acquisition to accelerate logistics growth.

Business Updates
- South West Port expanded to 12 MTPA.
- Mangalore terminal capacity 6 MTPA.
- Murbe Port approvals received.
- Kolkata terminal operations commenced.
- Arakkonam GCT commercialised.
- QIP of ₹7,503 crore completed.
- Moody's assigned Baa3 rating.

Balance Sheet
- Strong capital position.
- Expansion funded comfortably.
- Leverage expected to remain healthy.
- Investment-grade credit profile.

Impact
- Positive.
- Capacity expansion on track.
- Logistics business scaling rapidly.
- Long-term earnings visibility improving.

Key Takeaway
- JSW Infrastructure continues to execute an aggressive expansion strategy across ports and logistics. With a strong balance sheet, sizeable growth capex, improving logistics contribution and a target to more than double cargo handling capacity by FY30, the company remains well positioned for sustained long-term growth.

QMS MEDICAL: SUBSIDIARY RECEIVES SOC 2 TYPE II CERTIFICATION 🔐🏥

• 🏆 QMS Medical Allied Services announced that its subsidiary Saarathi Healthcare Pvt. Ltd. has received the SOC 2 Type II Certification based on the AICPA Trust Services Criteria, reaffirming its commitment to information security and data protection. 

• 🔒 The certification provides independent assurance on the design and operating effectiveness of controls across Security, Availability, Processing Integrity, Confidentiality and Privacy.  

• 💻 The assessment covered patient support operations, healthcare CRM platforms, digital workflow systems, cloud applications, data management processes, call centre operations, vendor management and information security controls. 

• ✅ The audit reported no major or minor non-conformities, confirming effective governance over healthcare information, cybersecurity, operational resilience and customer data management. 

🟢 Impact: Positive (The certification strengthens Saarathi Healthcare's credibility in healthcare technology and data security, enhancing customer trust and supporting future business opportunities.)

TAKYON NETWORKS: BAGS ₹2.62 CRORE ORDER FROM IIIT ALLAHABAD 🎓💻

• 📜 Takyon Networks has received a ₹2.62 crore work order from the Indian Institute of Information Technology (IIIT), Allahabad for SITC of ICT-enabled Audio-Visual Modernization of Classrooms. 

• 🏫 The project involves installation, testing and commissioning (SITC) of modern ICT-enabled audio-visual infrastructure to upgrade classrooms at IIIT Allahabad. 

• ⏳ The contract is to be completed within 16 weeks from the date of signing of the agreement and handover of the site. 

• 🤝 The order has been awarded by a domestic entity, with no promoter interest and is not a related-party transaction. 

🟢 Impact: Positive (The new order strengthens Takyon Networks' order book, enhances execution visibility and supports revenue growth.)

INDIAMART: TO SET UP WHOLLY-OWNED FINANCE SUBSIDIARY FOR MSME LENDING 💼🏦

• 🏢 IndiaMART's Board has approved the incorporation of a wholly owned subsidiary named IndiaMART Finance Limited, subject to necessary regulatory approvals. 

• 💰 The new subsidiary will operate in the financial services sector and aims to strengthen user trust, engagement and retention by providing short-term working capital and financing solutions to business users on the IndiaMART platform. 

• 💵 IndiaMART will invest ₹5 lakh as the initial paid-up capital by subscribing to 50,000 equity shares of ₹10 each, holding 100% ownership in the new company. 

• 📋 The incorporation is subject to approvals from the Ministry of Corporate Affairs and other applicable statutory/regulatory authorities. 

🟢 Impact: Positive (The finance subsidiary can deepen customer engagement, create new revenue opportunities and strengthen IndiaMART's MSME ecosystem.)

TCI: ₹81.96 CRORE INCOME TAX DEMAND REDUCED TO NIL ✅💰

• ⚖️ Transport Corporation of India (TCI) said the Income Tax Department has rectified computational errors in its assessment for AY 2024-25, following the company's rectification application. 

• 💰 The earlier income tax demand of ₹81.96 crore has been fully extinguished, with the outstanding demand now reduced to Nil after allowing appropriate TDS/TCS credits. 

• 📝 The Rectification Order under Section 154 of the Income Tax Act has been passed by the Jurisdictional Assessing Officer and given effect by the Centralized Processing Centre (CPC). 

• 📊 The company stated there is no outstanding tax demand for AY 2024-25 and no adverse financial, operational or other impact arising from the earlier assessment order. 

🟢 Impact: Positive (The elimination of the ₹81.96 crore tax demand removes a major overhang and strengthens TCI's financial position.)

COFORGE: NAMED 'EXCEPTIONAL PERFORMER' IN UK & IRELAND IT SOURCING STUDY 🏆☁️

• 🏅 Coforge has been recognized as an 'Exceptional Performer' in Application Services, Cloud & Infrastructure Services, and General Satisfaction in Whitelane Research's 2026 UK & Ireland IT Sourcing Study. It was also named a Strong Performer in Security Services. 

• 📊 In Application Services, Coforge ranked 2nd with a customer satisfaction score of 83%, outperforming the market average of 76%. 

• ☁️ In Cloud & Infrastructure Services, the company scored 83% versus the 75% market average, recording the largest year-on-year improvement in the category with a 10 percentage point increase. 

• 🌍 The recognition is based on feedback from nearly 400 participants and 1,000+ IT sourcing relationships across the UK & Ireland, marking the fourth consecutive year that Coforge has been acknowledged in the study. 

🟢 Impact: Positive (The recognition strengthens Coforge's brand credibility, enhances its competitive positioning in Europe and supports future client wins in digital and cloud services.)

VELOX SHIPPING: COMPLETES ACQUISITION OF 98% STAKE IN US LOGISTICS FIRM 🌍🚢

• 🤝 Velox Shipping & Logistics has completed the acquisition of a 98% stake in International Logistics Associates LLC (ILA), USA, after fulfilling all transaction conditions. 

• 💰 The company has paid the entire consideration of USD 447,860 (around US$0.45 million) for the acquisition, with the transaction completed on July 20, 2026.  

• 🌎 ILA is engaged in freight contracting, shipping agency and logistics services, and the acquisition is aimed at expanding Velox's international business operations, particularly in the United States.  

• 🤝 The acquisition is a cash transaction, is not a related-party transaction, and gives Velox operational presence in the global logistics market. 

🟢 Impact: Positive (The acquisition strengthens Velox's international footprint, expands its logistics capabilities in the US market and supports long-term growth.)

ANTONY WASTE: Q1 TONNAGE UP 5.4%, REFINANCES WTE LOAN AT LOWER RATE ♻️📈

• 🚛 Antony Waste managed ~1.40 million tonnes of waste in Q1 FY27, up ~5.4% YoY, while Collection & Transportation volumes grew ~5.1% and Processing volumes increased ~5.5%. Core revenue rose ~7% YoY. 

• 💰 The company refinanced the term loan of its Pimpri Chinchwad Waste-to-Energy (WtE) facility, reducing the interest rate from 10.25% to 8.25%, lowering borrowing costs by 200 bps and improving free cash flow. 

• ⚠️ Following the July 8, 2026 force majeure incident at the PCMC WtE facility, the company confirmed 9 employee fatalities, announced ₹25 lakh financial assistance per deceased employee, employment support for next of kin, and education support for minor children where required. 

• ♻️ The MRF and composting plant have resumed operations, while the company remains committed to the phased restart of the remaining WtE facility and will provide quarterly updates on recovery efforts. 

🟡 Impact: Mixed (Operational performance and lower financing costs are positive, but the tragic accident and phased restart of the WtE facility remain key near-term concerns.)

STEEL EXCHANGE INDIA: Q1 PAT JUMPS 47%, NET WORTH RISES TO ₹673 CRORE 📈🏗️

• 💰 Steel Exchange India reported Q1 FY27 net profit of ₹15.03 crore, up 46.89% YoY, while net profit margin improved to 6.0% from 3.0% a year ago. 

• 💵 The company's net worth increased to ₹672.76 crore, driven by preferential allotments and warrant conversions, significantly strengthening its balance sheet. 

• 📉 Debt-to-equity ratio improved to 0.27x from 0.48x YoY, while finance costs declined sharply, reflecting continued deleveraging. 

• ⚙️ The company improved its Asset Coverage Ratio to 3.77x and Interest Service Coverage Ratio to 2.52x, reinforcing its financial strength and liquidity. 

🟢 Impact: Positive (Strong profit growth, lower leverage and a strengthened balance sheet enhance financial stability and support long-term growth.)

CONNPLEX CINEMAS: Q1 OPERATIONAL NETWORK EXPANDS 63%, ADMITS RISE 36% 🎬📈

• 🎥 Connplex Cinemas expanded its network to 44 operational cinemas (+63% YoY), 122 screens (+72% YoY), 10,421 seats (+88% YoY), with presence across 32 cities in 10 states. 

• 👥 Admissions increased 36% YoY to 7.04 lakh, while the company signed 43 new screens during Q1 FY27, supporting future expansion. 

• 💰 Monetisation improved with Average Ticket Price (ATP) rising 7% YoY to ₹253 and Spend Per Head (SPH) increasing 6% YoY to ₹105, reflecting stronger pricing and higher F&B spending. 

• 🚀 The company remains focused on accelerating screen additions in underpenetrated markets and expects the 43 signed screens to support sustained long-term growth. 

🟢 Impact: Positive (Strong network expansion, higher footfalls and improving monetisation metrics strengthen Connplex's long-term growth outlook.)

RAJSHREE POLYPACK: SECURES ₹3.09 CRORE REPEAT ORDER FROM GLOBAL CONFECTIONERY MAJOR 📦🍫

• 📜 Rajshree Polypack has secured a repeat order worth approximately ₹3.09 crore from the wholly owned subsidiary of a global confectionery company known for 35+ iconic chocolate brands sold in over 170 countries. 

• 🏭 The company will supply Plastic Rigid Sheets under the contract, reinforcing its relationship with an existing customer. 

• ⏳ The order is scheduled to be executed by September 2026 or as mutually agreed between the parties. 

• 🤝 The contract is from a domestic entity, with no promoter interest and is not a related-party transaction. 

🟢 Impact: Positive (The repeat order strengthens customer confidence, enhances order book visibility and supports near-term revenue growth.)

MARUTI SUZUKI: TO HIKE CAR PRICES BY UP TO ₹30,000 FROM AUGUST 🚗📈

• 💰 Maruti Suzuki has announced a price increase of up to ₹30,000 across its vehicle portfolio, effective August 2026. 

• 📈 The price hike comes in response to the continuous rise in input costs and the prevailing inflationary cost environment. 

• ⚙️ The company said it has been implementing cost reduction measures over the past few months but is now constrained to pass on a portion of the increased costs to customers. 

• 🚘 The exact price increase will vary by model, with the company aiming to keep the customer impact to the minimum possible extent. 

🟢 Impact: Positive (The price hike should help Maruti Suzuki partially offset rising input costs and support profitability, although demand sensitivity remains a key monitorable.)

VIVIANA POWER TECH: COMMISSIONS 400 KV TRANSMISSION LINE FOR 323.4 MW WIND PARK ⚡🌬️

• ⚡ Viviana Power Tech has successfully commissioned a 400 kV Double-Circuit LILO transmission line for a 323.4 MW wind park in Kachchh, Gujarat, with the line being energised on July 10, 2026. 

• 🏗️ The project involved executing three high-voltage crossings, including a 765 kV under-crossing, ±500 kV HVDC under-crossing and a 400 kV overhead crossing, along with live-line stringing without a grid shutdown. 

• 📊 As of 21 July 2026, the company has a confirmed order book of ~₹1,340 crore (including BESS), bids under evaluation worth ~₹936 crore, and identified tenders exceeding ₹2,500 crore. 

• 🌱 The commissioned transmission line enables evacuation of renewable power from the wind park to the high-voltage grid, strengthening India's clean energy transmission infrastructure. 

🟢 Impact: Positive (The successful commissioning showcases Viviana's execution capabilities, strengthens its credentials in EHV transmission projects and enhances long-term growth visibility backed by a strong order pipeline.)

MACPOWER CNC: ACQUIRES 13 ACRES ON 30-YEAR LEASE FOR ₹50 CRORE EXPANSION 🏭🚀

• 🏗️ Macpower CNC Machines has acquired legally registered leasehold rights over ~13 acres of land (including 4 acres of green zone) near Metoda GIDC, Rajkot, on a 30-year lease basis. 

• 💰 The company plans to invest around ₹50 crore to develop manufacturing infrastructure, plant & machinery and utilities, with the project targeted for completion within 12 months. 

• ⚙️ The new facility will support debottlenecking of existing operations, backward integration, component manufacturing and future capacity expansion, building on the company's existing 2,500 machines per annum capacity. 

• 🎯 The project is eligible for benefits under the Viksit Gujarat Industrial Policy 2026, including up to 25% capital subsidy on plant & machinery/buildings and 7% interest subsidy. 

🟢 Impact: Positive (The strategic land acquisition and planned capacity expansion strengthen Macpower's long-term manufacturing capabilities, improve growth visibility and support future scale-up.)

GOVERNMENT SHORTLISTS 230 PRODUCTS ACROSS SECTORS FOR INDIGENISATION - ETNOW

A total of 230 products have been placed in the priority basket after analysing 335 products

The products have been shortlisted to boost domestic manufacturing and reduce import dependence amid global uncertainties

Categorised into three segments: Quicker Indigenisation, Scalable Indigenisation, and Aspirational Indigenisation

The indigenisation of prioritised products will be pursued through short-, medium-, and long-term plans

Working groups reviewed products across 25 ministries and departments

Alert: Government had constituted six working groups to identify products for indigenisation

Products from sectors such as medical devices, chemicals, textiles, automotive and EVs, capital goods, renewable energy, power, semiconductors, defence, electronics, space, and telecommunications, among others, have been prioritised

The working groups also identified key barriers to indigenisation

Support is likely to be provided in the form of PLI, demand guarantees, and subsidies based on the strategic plan

HCLTECH: REPORT HIGHLIGHTS AI ADOPTION–ROI GAP IN ENTERPRISES 🤖📊

• 📈 HCLTech's global research report, "The Blueprint for AI Leadership," found that 90% of enterprises say GenAI and Agentic AI are transforming workflows, with 91% reporting better data access and 90% seeing productivity gains. 

• 💰 Despite widespread AI adoption, only 18% of organizations report that AI is delivering significant revenue impact, highlighting a widening gap between AI implementation and business outcomes. 

• 🚀 The report says AI Leaders are 4x more likely to scale agentic and autonomous AI by focusing on measurable use cases, leadership sponsorship and enterprise-wide transformation. 

• 🎯 AI Leaders also lead in workforce readiness, with 93% having structured AI upskilling programs, compared with 20% of AI Followers, enabling stronger long-term competitive advantage. 

🟢 Impact: Positive (The report reinforces HCLTech's AI leadership and strengthens its positioning to benefit from increasing enterprise AI transformation spending.)

• 📍 The 4.5-acre project features 3 & 4 BHK apartments along with 14 exclusive duplex units, targeting premium homebuyers and long-term investors. 

• 🌿 Located near the Jayachamaraja Wadiyar Golf Club and Chamundi Hills, the project offers green views and excellent connectivity to commercial hubs, educational institutions, healthcare facilities, malls and hotels. 

• 📈 Brigade already has 26+ projects in Mysuru across residential, commercial and hospitality segments, and the new launch further strengthens its presence in the city.

MONEYBOXX FINANCE: RAISES ₹50 CRORE VIA NCDs, ₹70 CRORE IN 4 MONTHS 💰📈

• 💵 Moneyboxx Finance has raised ₹50 crore through Non-Convertible Debentures (NCDs), subscribed by Capri Global Capital, taking its total NCD fundraising to ₹70 crore in the first four months of FY27. 

• 🚀 The fresh capital will help the company expand lending in rural and semi-urban India, supporting small and micro entrepreneurs across key sectors. 

• 🏦 Moneyboxx is backed by 34 lenders, including State Bank of India, HDFC Bank and Kotak Mahindra Bank, and has raised over ₹1,500 crore of debt to date.

NHPC: 10 DEAD AFTER TUNNEL ACCIDENT AT 500 MW TEESTA-VI PROJECT ⚠️🏗️

• 🚨 An explosion caused by suspected methane gas trapped inside rocks occurred in the Head Race Tunnel (HRT) of the under-construction 500 MW Teesta Stage-VI Hydroelectric Project in Sikkim on 20 July 2026. 

• 🚑 NHPC immediately activated emergency response measures and is coordinating with NDRF, SDRF, district administration, DG Mines Safety and other agencies for rescue operations. 

• 👷 According to the latest update, 25 personnel were trapped, of which 10 fatalities have been reported so far, while evacuation and rescue efforts continue. 

• 🔍 NHPC said a detailed investigation will be conducted to determine the cause of the incident and the company is extending full support to the rescue agencies and affected families. 

🔴 Impact: Negative (The tragic accident may lead to project delays, regulatory scrutiny and increased focus on safety measures at the Teesta-VI project.)

AYE FINANCE: GUIDES 25–30% AUM GROWTH IN FY27, TARGETS ₹24,000 CR AUM IN 5 YEARS 📈🏦

• 📌 As of March 2026, AUM stood at ₹7,044 crore (+27% YoY) with 6.5 lakh active customers across 571 branches

• 🎯 Long-term ambition is to grow AUM to ~₹24,000 crore within five years, driven by deeper market penetration and expansion into products like gold loans and affordable housing

🟢 Impact: Positive (Strong growth guidance, improving profitability outlook and ambitious long-term expansion plan support positive business momentum.)

SUPREME INFRASTRUCTURE: ACHIEVES FINAL MILESTONE IN LENDER SETTLEMENT 🏗️💰

• 🏦 Supreme Infrastructure has achieved the final financial milestone for implementing its Scheme of Compromise and Arrangement with lenders. 

• 💵 With promoter support, the company has created a ₹120 crore Fixed Deposit with State Bank of India, sufficient to meet the balance settlement obligations to remaining lenders. 

• ⚖️ The Company Court has taken the Fixed Deposit on record and granted time until 17 August 2026 to complete the remaining banking and escrow formalities for full implementation of the scheme. 

• 🚀 The company expects the remaining process to be procedural, paving the way for completion of the restructuring scheme and supporting its long-term financial revival and value creation strategy. 

🟢 Impact: Positive (The funding milestone significantly advances the debt restructuring process, improves financial stability and supports the company's operational revival.)

*MSCI Aug'26 Review: Initial Cut-off Assessment after Day 1 | Biocon at a crucial juncture*

_Nuvama Alternative & Quant Research_

• The MSCI August review cut-off window commenced yesterday, and we are reiterating our assessment based on Day 1 of the cut-off period.

• Historically, MSCI has tended to determine the cut-off within the initial few days of the 10-working-day observation window.

• Our current view remains unchanged >>
* Three high-conviction inclusions
* Three high-conviction exclusions

• Biocon continues to be the only stock on the borderline. Based purely on Day 1 price cut-off, the stock missed the inclusion threshold by approximately 2.4%.

• If Biocon trades comfortably above ₹447 today and Day 2 is eventually chosen by MSCI as the cut-off date, the stock has the potential to qualify for inclusion.

• The actual cut-off date used by MSCI will only be known on August 13, when the review announcement is released.

*Key Dates*
* Cut-off Window: July 20 – July 31
* Announcement: August 13 (3:30 AM IST)
* Effective Date: August 31

**Paytm | CLSA View**

**Q1FY27 Highlights**
- EBITDA reported at **₹200 crore**.
- EBITDA marginally beat estimates.
- Payments GMV growth accelerated to **31% YoY**.
- Financial services revenue growth strengthened.

**Growth Drivers**
- Merchant payments gaining momentum.
- Financial services scaling well.
- Stable contribution margin at **55%**.
- Lower fixed costs boosted profitability.

**Key Monitorables**
- Payment take rate declined sequentially.
- Lower doubtful debt provisions aided EBITDA.
- Wallet license application submitted.
- Higher operating expenses expected ahead.

**Brokerage View**
- **CLSA:** **Outperform**.
- Target Price **₹1,050**.
- FY27–FY29 EBITDA estimates cut **2–3%**.
- Limited upside after recent stock rally.

**Key Takeaway**
- **CLSA acknowledges Paytm's strong operational momentum in payments and financial services, but believes much of the optimism around a possible UPI MDR return is already reflected in the stock price. While profitability continues to improve, higher operating expenses could moderate earnings growth.**

text: It was a PEAD stock around 900 rs couple of quarters back

ACCIDENT OF THE DAY : WAAREE, PREMIER ENERGIES 

*ALMM-II extension, -ve for Solar mfg, +ve for developers* 

 *What’s new?* 
- MNRE, on Saturday, announced limited extension to the implementation of ALMM List-II (Approved List of Models & Manufacturers for solar cells) for Net Metering and Open Access (OA) solar projects to Dec-26 (earlier: May-26). 
- The decision followed by industry concerns that domestic cell manufacturing capacity remains insufficient to meet near-term demand, potentially delaying projects and increasing costs.
- MNRE stated that the move is intended to facilitate a smoother transition towards domestic cell sourcing while protecting investments already made by developers and module manufacturers.
- Interestingly, the order specifically notes that the extension will also help module manufacturers monetize existing inventories and provide time for the domestic solar cell industry to scale up capacity before the full implementation of ALMM List-II.

 *Our view* 
 
1) Impact on Solar Module Manufacturers (Waaree Energies & Premier Energies)
Near-term impact: Negative
- This extension reduces the value creation opportunity for the manufacturers such as Waaree Energies and Premier Energies with stronger backward intergration
- As the industry manufacturing capacity expands and demand supply mismatch reduces over the next six months, the pricing power for early movers of cell capacity additon would erode for the DCR based modules
- On the non DCR module, there can be an increase in demand in Q2 and Q3, however, given the lower realization and margin compared to DCR modules, this would still be net negative on the FY27 financials of these companies.

2) *Impact on CleanMax* 
 *Near term impact: Positive* 
- For RE open access and rooftop developers like CleanMax, this could have a significant boost in the segment as there can be an increased demand from consumer to benefit from the capex cost savings
- For C&I developer, execution certainty is often more valuable than marginal equipment localization benefits, making this policy amendment an incremental positive for growth and project returns over the next six months.

 *3) Industry impact* 
- In our view, the strong solar capacity addition in FY26 was partly driven by capacity additions ramp up to benefit prior to the change in regulations.
- We earlier, therefore had anticipated the spurt of FY26 (44.6GW addition) to subside marginally in FY27 and estimated a 35GW addition for the year.
- However, the announced extension, we see upside risk to our estimate. BROKERAGE NOTE

**Asset Quality**
- Asset quality remained strong.
- Credit cost **0.7%**.
- Retail deposits improving.
- Fee income recovery expected.

**Brokerage View**
- **Jefferies:** Buy, TP **₹1,700**.
- FY27 EPS raised **2%**.
- Positive long-term outlook.

**Key Takeaway**
- **Jefferies remains positive on Axis Bank, supported by strong loan growth, healthy asset quality and improving retail deposits, with margin pressure expected to ease in 2HFY27.**

**HDFC Bank | Q1FY27 Jefferies Update**

**Financial Highlights**
- Profit **₹19,100 crore**.
- Up **5% YoY**.
- Results in line.
- Lower costs supported earnings.

**Business Performance**
- Loan growth **16% YoY**.
- Corporate lending increased.
- NIM down **12 bps QoQ**.
- NII growth **7%**.

**Profitability**
- Opex growth moderated.
- Credit costs declined.
- Low slippages supported profits.

**Brokerage View**
- **Jefferies:** Buy, TP **₹1,050**.
- PBT CAGR seen at **15%**.
- Positive long-term outlook.

**Key Takeaway**
- **Jefferies remains constructive on HDFC Bank, with strong loan growth, disciplined costs and lower credit costs supporting earnings despite continued margin compression from higher corporate lending.**

**HDFC Bank | Q1FY27 Nomura Update**

**Financial Highlights**
- Results in line.
- Margins declined QoQ.
- PAT met estimates.
- Credit costs stable.

**Business Performance**
- Loan growth accelerated.
- Deposit growth improved.
- Asset quality remained pristine.
- Growth momentum strengthened.

**Outlook**
- FCNR-led growth expected.
- Normalization continues.
- Valuation remains attractive.

**Brokerage View**
- **Nomura:** Buy, TP **₹950**.
- Positive on growth.
- Asset quality a key strength.

**Key Takeaway**
- **Nomura remains positive on HDFC Bank, expecting improving loan and deposit growth, pristine asset quality and FCNR-led acceleration despite near-term margin compression.**

**HDFC Bank | Q1FY27 Bernstein Update**

**Business Performance**
- Steady quarterly performance.
- Healthy balance-sheet growth.
- Asset quality remained resilient.
- Franchise normalization continued.

**Financial Highlights**
- Margin pressure persisted.
- Loan yields softened.
- Funding costs increased.
- NII growth remained constrained.

**Profitability**
- Operating efficiency supported.
- Stable asset quality cushioned.
- Profitability remained resilient.

**Brokerage View**
- **Bernstein:** Outperform, TP **₹1,150**.
- Positive on franchise quality.
- Margin headwinds persist.

**Key Takeaway**
- **Bernstein remains positive on HDFC Bank, citing healthy balance-sheet growth, resilient asset quality and strong operating efficiency, while noting that margin pressure continues to limit near-term earnings growth.**

**ICICI Bank | Q1FY27 Bernstein Update**

**Business Performance**
- Strongest loan growth.
- Deposit momentum improved.
- Industry-leading NIM.
- Profitability remained strong.

**Financial Highlights**
- Growth outpaced peers.
- Margins stayed resilient.
- RoA reached **2.5%**.
- Earnings quality remained high.

**Brokerage View**
- **Bernstein:** Market Perform, TP **₹1,550**.
- Best-performing large private bank.
- Balanced valuation view.

**Key Takeaway**
- **Bernstein views ICICI Bank as the best-performing large private bank this quarter, driven by superior loan growth, strong deposit traction, industry-leading margins and a sector-leading 2.5% RoA, while maintaining a Market Perform rating on valuation.**

**ICICI Bank | Q1FY27 Nomura Update**

**Financial Highlights**
- PAT beat estimates **6%**.
- Margins remained resilient.
- Strong earnings quality.
- Outperformed expectations.

**Business Performance**
- Sector-leading loan growth.
- Loan growth re-accelerated.
- Asset quality remained pristine.
- Margin resilience continued.

**Earnings Outlook**
- FY27-28 EPS raised **5%**.
- Growth outlook strengthened.
- Valuation remains reasonable.

**Brokerage View**
- **Nomura:** Buy, TP **₹1,700**.
- Positive on margins.
- Favors strong growth outlook.

**Key Takeaway**
- **Nomura remains bullish on ICICI Bank, citing sector-leading loan growth, resilient margins, pristine asset quality and a quality earnings beat despite a challenging margin environment.**

**Private Banks | Q1FY27 Morgan Stanley Update**

**ICICI Bank**
- Strongest loan growth.
- NIM expanded.
- Fee income remained strong.
- Best operating performance.

**HDFC Bank**
- NII missed estimates.
- Higher NIM compression.
- Margins remained under pressure.

**Axis Bank**
- NII missed estimates.
- NIM compressed further.
- Corporate mix reduced spreads.

**Kotak Mahindra Bank**
- NIM met estimates.
- Results beat forecasts.
- Overall performance solid.

**Key Takeaway**
- **Morgan Stanley prefers ICICI Bank for its superior loan growth and margin expansion, while Kotak delivered a positive surprise. HDFC Bank and Axis Bank faced pressure from higher-than-expected NIM compression.**

**JSW Steel | Q1FY27 Citi Update**

**Financial Highlights**
- EBITDA **₹9,370 crore**.
- Up **32% YoY**.
- Beat estimates by **14%**.
- Higher realizations drove growth.
- India EBITDA **₹15,110/ton**.

**Operational Highlights**
- Domestic volumes up **~1% YoY**.
- Weak retail and longs.
- Q2 volumes expected higher.
- Auto price hikes reflected.

**Cost & Margin Outlook**
- Q2 prices may soften.
- Coking coal costs peaked.
- Lower ore costs expected.
- Better operating leverage ahead.

**Balance Sheet**
- Net Debt/EBITDA **1.5x**.
- Comfortable below **2.5x**.

**Brokerage View**
- **Citi:** Sell, TP **₹1,100**.
- Sees EBITDA peak in Q1.
- Valuation remains expensive.

**Key Takeaway**
- **Despite a strong Q1 driven by higher realizations and improved margins, Citi remains cautious, expecting softer pricing after Q1 and believing EBITDA has likely peaked for the near term.**

**JSW Steel | Q1FY27 Jefferies Update**

**Financial Highlights**
- EBITDA up **38% YoY**.
- Beat estimates by **20%**.
- Fifth straight double-digit growth.
- Strong operating momentum.

**Earnings Outlook**
- FY27-28 EPS raised **1-4%**.
- EPS may treble FY26-29.
- FY27 EBITDA seen improving.
- Profitability outlook strong.

**Growth Outlook**
- Capacity target **80 MT** by FY32.
- Potential top-3 global producer.
- Expansion remains on track.
- Long-term growth intact.

**Brokerage View**
- **Jefferies:** Buy, TP **₹1,650**.
- Top India Metals pick.
- Positive long-term outlook.

**Key Takeaway**
- **Jefferies remains highly bullish on JSW Steel, citing strong earnings momentum, rising profitability and an aggressive capacity expansion plan that could make it one of the world's top three steel producers by FY32.**

**JSW Steel | Q1FY27 Goldman Sachs Update**

**Operational Highlights**
- India sales **6.3 MT**.
- Volumes up **5.5% YoY**.
- Growth despite BF-3 outage.
- Retail demand remained weak.

**Balance Sheet**
- Net debt down **₹7,700 crore QoQ**.
- BPSL divestiture completed.
- Net Debt/EBITDA **1.46x**.
- Well below **2.5x** threshold.

**Growth Outlook**
- BF-3 furnace commissioned.
- Higher Q2 volumes guided.
- Expansion capex on track.
- Downstream projects support margins.
- H2FY27 volume recovery expected.

**Brokerage View**
- **Goldman Sachs:** Buy, TP **₹1,525**.
- Positive despite softer Q2 spreads.
- Volume growth to offset pressure.

**Key Takeaway**
- **Goldman Sachs remains bullish on JSW Steel, supported by strong deleveraging, higher production after BF-3 commissioning and capacity expansion, with H2FY27 volume growth expected to offset softer near-term spreads.**

**JSW Steel | Q1FY27 Morgan Stanley Update**

**Financial Highlights**
- EBITDA beat expectations.
- Better realizations supported.
- Strong cost control.
- Volumes remained softer.

**Balance Sheet**
- BPSL proceeds received.
- Further deleveraging achieved.
- Financial position strengthened.

**Outlook**
- Q2FY27 seasonally weak.
- Near-term softness expected.
- Long-term outlook intact.

**Brokerage View**
- **Morgan Stanley:** Overweight, TP **₹1,470**.
- EBITDA exceeded estimates.
- Positive beyond seasonality.

**Key Takeaway**
- **Morgan Stanley remains positive on JSW Steel as better realizations, disciplined cost control and continued deleveraging outweighed weaker volumes, with investors advised to look beyond the seasonally soft Q2.**

**JSW Steel | Q1FY27 JPMorgan Update**

**Financial Highlights**
- EBITDA grew **32% YoY**.
- India business remained strong.
- US Ohio performed well.
- Italy operations stayed robust.

**Balance Sheet**
- Net Debt/EBITDA **~1.5x**.
- Down from **3.2x YoY**.
- Target below **2.5x**.
- Capex remains on track.

**Outlook**
- Spreads may moderate in Q2.
- Raw material costs peaked.
- Recovery expected in 2HFY27.
- Margin outlook constructive.

**Brokerage View**
- **JPMorgan:** Overweight, TP **₹1,500** *(raised)*.
- Top pick in India Metals.
- Positive long-term outlook.

**Key Takeaway**
- **JPMorgan remains highly positive on JSW Steel, citing strong global operations, a much stronger balance sheet and expectations of margin recovery in 2HFY27 despite near-term spread moderation.**

**Reliance Industries | Brokerage Update**

**Brokerage Ratings**
- Goldman Sachs – Buy | TP ₹1,870.
- CLSA – Outperform | TP ₹1,800.
- Nomura – Buy | TP ₹1,690.
- Motilal Oswal – Buy | TP ₹1,550.
- Macquarie – Outperform | TP ₹1,510.
- Morgan Stanley – Q1 earnings beat estimates.

**Key Positives**
- Strong O2C and energy performance.
- Jio delivered steady growth.
- Retail recovery remains a key trigger.
- New Energy ramp-up in focus.

**Key Takeaway**
- **Brokerages remain positive on Reliance Industries after a Q1FY27 earnings beat, supported by strong O2C, resilient Jio performance and improving retail and New Energy outlook.**

**JSW Steel | Q1FY27 Kotak Institutional Equities Update**

**Financial Highlights**
- EBITDA beat estimates.
- Higher realizations supported earnings.
- India EBITDA **₹15,110/ton**.
- Margins up **23.3% QoQ**.

**Margin Outlook**
- Margins may soften in Q2.
- Seasonal pressure on longs.
- Coal costs remain elevated.
- Higher volumes partly offset.

**Growth & Expansion**
- BPSL JV transaction completed.
- Capacity target **49 MTPA** by FY30.
- Expansion pipeline remains strong.
- Margin-accretive projects underway.

**Brokerage View**
- **Kotak Institutional Equities:** **Upgrade to Buy**, TP **₹1,450**.
- Recent correction attractive.
- Positive long-term outlook.

**Key Takeaway**
- **Kotak upgraded JSW Steel to Buy after a stronger-than-expected quarter, citing healthy realizations, robust expansion plans and an attractive risk-reward despite near-term margin pressure.**

**Reliance Industries | Q1FY27 MOSL Update**

**Financial Highlights**
- Energy profitability rebounded.
- Offset weaker Retail performance.
- Overall earnings remained resilient.

**Retail Business**
- Soft Q1FY27 performance.
- EBITDA to double in three years.
- Recovery remains key trigger.

**Jio Business**
- Results broadly in line.
- JPL IPO key monitorable.
- Telecom outlook remains stable.

**Growth Triggers**
- Retail recovery.
- FMCG scale-up.
- New Energy expansion.
- AI & Datacentre growth.

**Brokerage View**
- **MOSL:** Buy, TP **₹1,550**.
- Positive long-term outlook.
- Multiple growth catalysts.

**Key Takeaway**
- **MOSL remains constructive on Reliance, expecting recovery in Retail, resilient Jio performance and scaling of FMCG, New Energy, AI and Datacentre businesses to drive long-term value creation.**

**Reliance Industries | Q1FY27 Morgan Stanley Update**

**Financial Highlights**
- Earnings beat Street estimates.
- Quality of earnings improved.
- Refining business outperformed.
- Chemicals remained strong.

**Consumer Business**
- Retail revenue met estimates.
- Consumer growth remained steady.
- Execution stayed on track.

**New Energy**
- Polysilicon execution accelerated.
- Solar panel progress continued.
- Battery storage scaling.
- Manufacturing ramp-up underway.

**Brokerage View**
- **Morgan Stanley:** Positive.
- Refining drove earnings beat.
- New energy execution impressed.

**Key Takeaway**
- **Morgan Stanley highlighted a quality earnings beat led by refining and chemicals, while steady retail growth and faster execution in the solar and battery manufacturing ecosystem strengthen Reliance's long-term outlook.**

**Reliance Industries | Q1FY27 Macquarie Update**

**Business Performance**
- O2C earnings strengthened.
- Jio growth remained robust.
- Retail margins under pressure.
- Overall quarter remained strong.

**Jio & Retail**
- Jio compounding continues.
- Digital outlook improving.
- Retail EBITDA upside likely.
- Execution remains healthy.

**Retail Outlook**
- EBITDA to double in three years.
- Management reiterated target.
- Margin recovery expected.

**Brokerage View**
- **Macquarie:** Outperform, TP **₹1,510**.
- Positive on Jio.
- Constructive on Retail.
- Earnings upside possible.

**Key Takeaway**
- **Macquarie sees upside to consensus driven by sustained Jio growth, stronger digital services and improving retail EBITDA, despite near-term retail margin pressure.**

**Reliance Industries | Q1FY27 CLSA Update**

**Financial Highlights**
- EBITDA beat estimates **3%**.
- PAT beat estimates **20%**.
- O2C outperformed expectations.
- Higher other income supported.

**Business Performance**
- Retail missed estimates.
- Interest costs increased.
- FMCG growth remained strong.
- Media momentum continued.

**Retail Outlook**
- Retail growth subdued FY27.
- EBITDA to double in three years.
- Long-term outlook positive.

**Growth Triggers**
- New energy ramp-up.
- O2C strength continues.
- Retail KPIs improving.
- Media & FMCG value unlocking.

**Brokerage View**
- **CLSA:** Outperform, TP **₹1,800**.
- Positive on long-term growth.
- Multiple catalysts ahead.

**Key Takeaway**
- **CLSA remains positive on Reliance as strong O2C performance, resilient FMCG and media growth, retail EBITDA expansion and new energy ramp-up provide multiple long-term growth triggers.**

**Reliance Industries | Q1FY27 Nomura Update**

**O2C Business**
- EBITDA **₹17,000 crore**.
- Up **17% QoQ**.
- Four-year high.
- Crack spreads boosted earnings.

**E&P Business**
- EBITDA **₹5,000 crore**.
- Up **19% QoQ**.
- Strong recovery.

**Jio Telecom**
- Revenue **₹34,200 crore**.
- Up **2.5% QoQ**.
- Subscriber growth continued.
- ARPU improved.

**Retail Business**
- Revenue **₹90,400 crore**.
- Missed estimates **4%**.
- Down **8% QoQ**.
- Margin recovery key.

**Brokerage View**
- **Nomura:** Buy, TP **₹1,690**.
- Energy businesses outperformed.
- Retail remains watchpoint.

**Key Takeaway**
- **Strong energy-led quarter with record O2C earnings, improving E&P, steady Jio growth and retail margin recovery remaining the key monitorable.**

**Reliance Industries | Q1FY27 GS Update**

**O2C Business**
- Core EBITDA broadly in line.
- O2C EBITDA up **17% QoQ**.
- Performance beat market expectations.
- Outlook improves for Q2FY27.

**O2C Drivers**
- Higher crude premiums impacted.
- Logistics costs remained elevated.
- Fuel marketing under-recoveries.
- Windfall taxes weighed.
- Planned refinery turnaround impacted.

**Retail Business**
- EBITDA margin down **80 bps YoY**.
- Higher digital commerce investments.
- Hyperlocal delivery expansion continued.
- Margins faced near-term pressure.

**New Energy**
- Solar PV plant nearing commissioning.
- Battery manufacturing progressing.
- Integrated manufacturing platform advancing.
- New energy execution on track.

**Brokerage View**
- **Goldman Sachs:** Buy, TP **₹1,870**.
- Core performance met expectations.
- O2C outlook turning constructive.
- Positive long-term investment view.

**Key Takeaway**
- **Reliance delivered a steady quarter with resilient O2C performance, improving outlook for refining, continued investments in retail and meaningful progress in its new energy business. Goldman Sachs remains positive with a Buy rating and ₹1,870 target price.**

**JSW Steel | Q1FY27 Update**

**Financial Highlights**
- Adjusted EBITDA/ton **₹14,990**.
- Up from **₹12,232 QoQ**.
- Operating profitability improved.
- Earnings remained strong.

**Balance Sheet**
- Net debt **₹46,157 crore**.
- Down from **₹53,870 crore**.
- Leverage reduced significantly.
- Balance sheet strengthened.

**Overseas Operations**
- US EBITDA **US$15.9 million**.
- Italy EBITDA **€7.1 million**.
- Overseas business turned profitable.
- Operational performance improved.

**Brokerage Views**
- **Jefferies:** Buy, TP **₹1,650**.
- **Motilal Oswal:** Buy, TP **₹1,470**.
- **Nuvama:** Reduce, TP **₹1,172**.
- Consensus remains constructive.

**Growth Outlook**
- Capacity target **80 MT** by FY32.
- EPS expected to treble FY26–FY29.
- Margin outlook remains healthy.
- Long-term expansion continues.

**Key Takeaway**
- **JSW Steel delivered a strong quarter with higher EBITDA per tonne, sharp debt reduction and a turnaround in overseas operations. While brokerages differ on valuation, most remain positive on the company's long-term earnings growth, capacity expansion and improving profitability.**

**Jyoti CNC | Investment Snapshot**

**Brokerage View**
- YES Securities TP **₹1,075**.
- Sunidhi TP **₹1,250**.
- Nirmal Bang TP **₹976**.
- Upside potential **34–80%**.
- All maintain **Buy** ratings.

**Growth Drivers**
- Record order book **₹4,732 crore**.
- Revenue visibility **18–24 months**.
- Orders up **30%+ YoY**.
- Engineering orders up **58%**.
- Auto orders up **23%**.
- Defence orders up **23%**.

**Capacity Expansion**
- Capacity tripling underway.
- Current **6,000 machines/year**.
- Target **16,000 machines/year**.
- Expansion by **Sep-2026**.
- Plants operating above capacity.

**Financial Strength**
- FY26 revenue **₹1,949 crore**.
- Revenue growth **20% YoY**.
- EBITDA margin **28.9%**.
- Q4 EBITDA margin **31.9%**.
- Gross margin above **50%**.
- PAT **₹391 crore**.
- Street expects **45% PAT CAGR**.

**Technology & Opportunities**
- Developing proprietary CNC controllers.
- In-house servo drives and motors.
- AI and nano-precision capabilities.
- Semiconductor equipment opportunity.
- Import substitution potential.

**Industry Tailwinds**
- EMS demand rising sharply.
- Defence capex accelerating.
- CNC replacement cycle beginning.
- India imports ~60% CNC machines.
- Domestic manufacturing gaining share.

**French Probe**
- Probe limited to Huron subsidiary.
- Revenue impact was deferred.
- No order cancellations reported.
- India operations unaffected.
- Management sees normal operations.

**Key Risks**
- Revenue missed guidance.
- Working capital remains elevated.
- Huron impacted consolidated PAT.
- French probe uncertainty persists.
- Promoter pledge at **15.4%**.
- No dividend payout.

**Key Takeaway**
- **Despite a sharp correction, Jyoti CNC retains strong long-term growth drivers through a record order book, aggressive capacity expansion, localisation of critical technologies and favourable industry tailwinds. However, investors should closely monitor execution, working capital, Huron-related developments and delivery of management's growth guidance.**

**Kusumgar | Business & Valuation View**

**Business Profile**
- Specialized technical fabrics manufacturer.
- Around **50%** revenue from aerospace & defence.
- Operates in a niche segment.
- No listed domestic peer.

**Brokerage View**
- Emkay has initiated coverage.
- Target price: **₹800**.
- Valuation benchmarked to aerospace & defence peers.

**Valuation Perspective**
- Business remains primarily a textile manufacturer.
- Aerospace exposure enhances growth prospects.
- Premium valuation driven by niche positioning.
- Current valuation leaves limited margin of safety.

**Investment Considerations**
- IPO price of **₹419** offered reasonable valuation.
- Current price around **₹580** appears more demanding.
- Small-cap bull market could support premium multiples.
- Lack of listed peers may sustain valuation premium.

**Key Risks**
- Elevated valuation risk.
- Multiple contraction if sentiment weakens.
- Execution of growth plans remains critical.

**Key Takeaway**
- **Kusumgar offers a unique niche play in specialized technical fabrics with significant exposure to the aerospace & defence sector. While its differentiated business may justify a valuation premium, the sharp post-listing rally has reduced the margin of safety, making future returns increasingly dependent on sustained earnings execution rather than further valuation expansion.**

**Unimech Aerospace | Business Update**

**Core Business**
- Derives ~90% of revenue from aerospace tooling, supplying critical tools for Airbus, Boeing, Rolls-Royce, Pratt & Whitney and LEAP engine programs.
- 89% of revenue comes from exports.

**Diversification Push**
- Expanding beyond aerospace into nuclear, defence, semiconductors, energy and oil & gas through organic growth and acquisitions.

**Key Developments**
- Order book surged from ₹93 Cr to ₹314 Cr between Mar'25 and May'26.
- Won a ₹72 Cr NPCIL order for critical equipment in India's nuclear sector.
- Formed a 51:49 JV with Kanoo Group in Saudi Arabia to tap oil & gas, utilities and mining opportunities.
- Acquired Hobel Bellows for ₹450 Cr, adding metallic bellows, tubing and precision assemblies to its portfolio.
- Increased stake in Dheya Engineering to 30%, gaining exposure to micro gas turbines and defence applications.

**Operations**
- Operates through 3 manufacturing facilities with 2.73 lakh sq ft of manufacturing space.
- 5,500+ SKUs across 35 customers in 7 countries.

**Key Takeaway**
- **Unimech Aerospace is leveraging its precision engineering expertise to build a multi-sector presence across high-entry-barrier industries, transforming from a pure-play aerospace tooling company into a diversified precision engineering powerhouse with significantly larger addressable opportunities.**
